varieties.name: Fordhook Giant
varieties.origin: USA, 1924, introduced by W. Atlee Burpee & Company
varieties.traits: Large, glossy, heavily savoyed dark green leaves; broad flat white stems up to 2.5 inches wide
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant producer, vigorous growth throughout the season

varieties.name: Lucullus
varieties.traits: Pale celery-green to white stems; heavily crumpled savoyed dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Highly prolific; 3-4 plants supply most families
varieties.growing_notes: Exceptional heat AND cold tolerance; ideal for Brisbane's variable climate; continuous harvesting over long season; widely considered best-tasting chard; Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit
varieties.availability: Available from major Australian nurseries and seed companies

varieties.name: Rainbow Chard (Five Color Mix)
varieties.origin: Mix developed by Diggers Seeds (Australia); combines traditional colored varieties
varieties.traits: Multicolored stems in gold, pink, orange, crimson, white, purple; green and bronze leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Productive; slightly less vigorous than white-stemmed varieties
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane gardens; tolerates subtropical heat with regular watering; adds color to gardens; grown at Epicurious Southbank Brisbane demonstration garden; Award winner from Diggers Seeds
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, major Australian retailers

varieties.name: Bright Lights
varieties.origin: Developed by Johnny's Selected Seeds; 1998 All-America Selections Winner
varieties.traits: Electric rainbow of stems: yellow, gold, orange, pink, red, purple, white, green, striped
varieties.yield_notes: Good production; consistent growth rate across all colors
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Brisbane but somewhat less frost-hardy than others; strong bolt resistance; excellent ornamental; Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit; maintains color after light cooking

varieties.name: Ruby Red (Rhubarb Chard)
varieties.origin: Heirloom introduced to USA in 1857; mentioned by Aristotle in 350 BC
varieties.traits: Brilliant candy-apple red/crimson stalks and veins; dark green heavily savoyed leaves

varieties.name: Golden Sunrise
varieties.traits: Deep golden-yellow to orange stalks, midribs and veins; shiny jade-green slightly savoyed leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical conditions; good heat tolerance; frost-tolerant; wonderful color adds visual appeal; never tough or bitter

varieties.name: Peppermint
varieties.origin: Hybrid developed by Johnny's Selected Seeds/Sakata
varieties.traits: Bright candy-striped stems: vivid pink-on-white vertical stripes; glossy dark green heavily savoyed leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Long, strong petioles; upright habit makes harvesting easy
varieties.growing_notes: Highly bolt-resistant and disease-resistant; excellent for Brisbane containers; particularly heat-tolerant; ace of field trials; strong performance in humid conditions

varieties.name: Perpetual Spinach (Leaf Beet)
varieties.traits: Smooth, flat, dark green leaves resembling spinach; thin white to pale green midribs
varieties.yield_notes: Almost perpetual harvest; extremely productive in small spaces
varieties.growing_notes: Exceptional heat tolerance for Brisbane summers; survives 100°F temperatures with minimal care; biennial so rarely bolts first year; drought-resistant with deep taproot; significantly lower oxalic acid than standard chard (sweeter, milder); can survive several years in garden

varieties.name: Barese (Cicla di Bari)
varieties.origin: Italian heirloom from Puglia region, named after city of Bari
varieties.traits: Glossy dark green leaves with slightly curled edges; thick bright white stems
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane; slow to bolt; remarkably sweet even when mature; perfect for baby leaf or full-size; tolerates summer heat better than spinach; resilient to pests and harsh weather despite tender texture

varieties.name: Large White Ribbed
varieties.traits: Wide, flattened white stems; large broad leaves

varieties.name: Argentata (Bionda da Costa)
varieties.origin: Italian heirloom, revered by Italian cooks
varieties.traits: Luminous silvery-white stems; large, deep green savoyed leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Brisbane; more cold-hardy than most; juicy white stalks never stringy or tough; excellent flavor when steamed; adds fairy-tale ambience to garden with silvery stems

varieties.name: Magenta Sunset
varieties.traits: Hot pink to magenta stems and veins; dark jade-colored foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Tends to bolt in Brisbane summer heat; best grown for immature salad greens in hot weather or planted in cooler months; stunning ornamental value

varieties.name: Flamingo Pink
varieties.traits: Bright flamingo-pink stalks; ruffled green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Good production for baby greens (30 days) and full-grown (60 days)
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Brisbane; handles cool temperatures and still thrives; perfect for early spring or fall growing

varieties.name: Charbell F1
varieties.origin: Hybrid developed by Johnny's Selected Seeds
varieties.traits: Brilliant deep magenta stems and veins; glossy dark green heavily textured leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Fast-growing; vigorous plants with upright habit
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane; very bolt-resistant unlike traditional magenta chards; strong Cercospora resistance; bred for baby leaf but performs well at full size; wide petioles make harvesting easy

varieties.name: Red Sensation
varieties.traits: Stunning deep red stems; green foliage with red veins
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptionally reliable; popular with salad growers
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical conditions; can be grown all year round in tropical regions; ideal for container gardens; very ornamental

varieties.name: Vulcan
varieties.traits: Rich rhubarb-red stems; curly dark green leaves with purple tinge
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Brisbane; frost-tolerant; performs well in cooler weather; best in soil temperatures 50-75°F

varieties.name: Perpetual Spinach (Leaf Beet) 🏆 - HIGHLY RECOMMENDED
varieties.traits: Smooth, dark green leaves resembling spinach; thin white midribs
varieties.growing_notes: EXCEPTIONAL for Kerala - survives extreme heat over 100°F with minimal care; biennial so rarely bolts in first year; drought-resistant with deep taproot; significantly lower oxalic acid; can be grown year-round in Tampa (similar climate to Kerala); produces continuously through hot, humid conditions; excellent substitute for spinach in South Indian dishes
varieties.availability: International specialty seed suppliers; worth importing for Kerala conditions

varieties.name: Fordhook Giant
varieties.traits: Large, glossy, heavily savoyed dark green leaves; broad white stems

varieties.name: Lucullus
varieties.traits: Pale green/white stems; heavily savoyed dark green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Exceptional heat tolerance; widely considered best-tasting chard; flavor improves in cooler weather; recommended in Southern US for heat tolerance; suitable for Kerala with afternoon shade

varieties.name: Barese (Cicla di Bari) 🏆 - HIGHLY RECOMMENDED
varieties.traits: Glossy dark green leaves; thick white stems; compact 10-18 inches
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala heat; slow to bolt; tolerates temperatures in high 80s-90s°F; resilient to pests despite tender texture; perfect for succession planting; remarkably sweet even when mature; best harvested at 7-9 inches

varieties.name: Rainbow Chard (Five Color Mix)
varieties.traits: Multicolored stems: gold, pink, orange, crimson, white, purple

varieties.name: Bright Lights
varieties.traits: Electric rainbow of stems in multiple colors
varieties.yield_notes: Good production with strong bolt resistance
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Kerala but less frost-hardy (not a concern); good bolt resistance helps in tropical heat; vigorous producer in all climates; plant during cooler periods

varieties.name: Peppermint 🧬 - HIGHLY RECOMMENDED
varieties.traits: Bright candy-striped pink-and-white stems; glossy dark green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala - highly bolt-resistant and heat-tolerant; strong disease resistance crucial for humid monsoon climate; specifically noted as heat-tolerant; performs abundantly in almost any climate; upright habit helps air circulation in humid conditions

varieties.name: Charbell F1
varieties.traits: Brilliant deep magenta stems; heavily textured dark green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Very bolt-resistant - crucial for Kerala conditions; strong Cercospora resistance (common in humid climates); performs well at full size despite being bred for baby leaf; wide petioles

varieties.name: Red Sensation
varieties.traits: Deep red stems; green foliage with red veins; 40-50 cm
varieties.growing_notes: CAN BE GROWN ALL YEAR ROUND IN TROPICAL REGIONS - specifically noted; excellent for containers; very ornamental; reliable in tropical heat
varieties.availability: May be available through Indian seed retailers

varieties.name: Large White Ribbed
varieties.traits: Wide, flattened white stems; smooth leaves

varieties.name: Ruby Red (Rhubarb Chard)
varieties.traits: Brilliant red stalks; dark green savoyed leaves; 20-24 inches

varieties.name: Golden Sunrise
varieties.traits: Deep golden-yellow stalks; jade-green leaves; 16-24 inches
varieties.growing_notes: Good heat tolerance; suitable for Kerala with consistent moisture; beautiful golden color adds appeal; frost-tolerant (not relevant for Kerala)
varieties.availability: International suppliers; some availability in India

varieties.name: Magenta Sunset
varieties.traits: Hot pink to magenta stems; dark jade leaves; 24 inches
varieties.growing_notes: Tends to bolt during extreme heat; best grown for baby salad greens in Kerala's hot season or planted during cooler post-monsoon period; stunning ornamental value

varieties.name: Argentata (Bionda da Costa)
varieties.traits: Silvery-white stems; large deep green savoyed leaves; up to 36 inches
varieties.growing_notes: More cold-hardy than heat-adapted; best for Kerala during cooler months; juicy white stalks never stringy; excellent when steamed

varieties.name: Flamingo Pink
varieties.traits: Bright flamingo-pink stalks; ruffled green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Good for baby greens (30 days) and full-grown (60 days)
varieties.growing_notes: Handles cool temperatures; suitable for Kerala cooler season planting; perfect for succession planting during favorable weather

varieties.name: Vulcan
varieties.traits: Rich rhubarb-red stems; curly dark green purple-tinged leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Performs best in 50-75°F; suitable for Kerala cooler months; improved over standard rhubarb chard for bolt resistance
